risk_summary: The domain github.com has a robust security posture with several security headers enabled, but there are some potential issues with subdomain configuration and HTTP header settings. Overall, the risk is relatively low, but some improvements can be made to further enhance security.

findings.title: X-XSS-Protection Header Set to 0
findings.description: The X-XSS-Protection header is set to 0, which disables the browser's built-in XSS protection. This could make the application more vulnerable to XSS attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set the X-XSS-Protection header to 1; mode=block to enable the browser's built-in XSS protection.

findings.title: Unsecured Subdomains
findings.description: Some subdomains (e.g., admin.github.com, alive-staging.github.com, developer.github.com, developers.github.com, docs-internal.github.com) have different IP addresses, which could indicate unsecured or unused subdomains.
findings.recommendation: Review and secure or remove unused subdomains to prevent potential security risks.

findings.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy (CSP) Directives
findings.description: The Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header is present, but some directives (e.g., 'upgrade-insecure-requests') are not explicitly defined.
findings.recommendation: Review and update the CSP header to include all necessary directives to ensure optimal security.

findings.title: Secure Cookie Flags
findings.description: The Set-Cookie header sets the 'secure' flag, which is good practice for securing cookies.
findings.recommendation: Continue to use the 'secure' flag for all cookies to ensure their confidentiality and integrity.
